rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible ports or explicitly declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions; avoid direct references to AADL properties.
generic_rules: Contracts should express observable interface invariants, not internal implementation details.
Boolean logic in guarantees should be grounded in named, visible Boolean ports or derived equations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE blocks must be wrapped as: annex agree {** ... **};
Use const or eq to expose derived values before referencing them in assume/guarantee clauses.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without mediation via outputs or equations.
